stories.headline: US Justice Department drops criminal probe of Fed chair Jerome Powell
stories.summary: The announcement on Friday is expected to clear the path for the confirmation of his successor, Kevin Warsh.

stories.headline: US justice department drops probe into Fed chairman Jerome Powell
stories.summary: President Donald Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed's building.

stories.headline: US prosecutors drop criminal probe into Fed chair Powell
stories.summary: Breakthrough removes potential hurdle to Kevin Warsh’s confirmation as next US central bank chief

stories.headline: Witkoff and Kushner to travel to Islamabad as hopes rise for Iran peace talks
stories.summary: US delegation wants to ‘hear Iranians out’ as Tehran’s foreign minister also confirms trip to Pakistan’s capital

stories.headline: DOJ Drops Powell Probe, Iran War Impact on Credit Markets | Real Yield 4/24/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg Real Yield" highlights the market-moving news you need to know. Today's guests: BMO Capital Markets Head of US Rates Strategy Ian Lyngen, Moody's Rating Global Head of Private Credit Marc Pinto, Former Federal Reserve General Counsel Scott Alvarez, Barings High Yield Portfolio Manager Michael Best, and Neuberger Head of Private Debt Susan Kasser.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Witkoff, Kushner Heading to Pakistan for More Talks
stories.summary: President Donald Trump is sending special envoy Steve Witkoff and his son-in-law Jared Kushner to Pakistan to hold another round of talks with Iran. This as Israel and Lebanon extend their own ceasefire. Bloomberg's Dan Williams has more from Jerusalem. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Treasuries Gain as DOJ Drops Fed Probe, Opening Path for Warsh
stories.summary: Treasuries edged higher after the Justice Department dropped its investigation into the Federal Reserve, potentially opening a path for President Donald Trump’s pick Kevin Warsh to become Fed chair and push for interest-rate cuts.

stories.headline: How Israel targeted and killed Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil
stories.summary: Amal Khalil was killed while reporting on an Israeli air strike in southern Lebanon during a 10-day ceasefire.

stories.headline: Iran-Iraq Tanker War redux? Why the Strait of Hormuz crisis is different
stories.summary: The Tanker War between Iran and Iraq in the 1980s also disrupted global shipping. But the situation today is different.

stories.headline: US envoys Witkoff, Kushner to head to Pakistan for Iran talks on Saturday, White House says
stories.summary: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is expected to arrive in Islamabad on Friday night, an official source in Pakistan said, without providing details about who he was likely to meet.

stories.headline: Nato says 'no provision' to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain
stories.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war.

stories.headline: No 10 says Falklands sovereignty rests with UK after report of US 'review'

stories.summary: The Pentagon’s rush to rearm its Mideast forces makes it less ready to confront potential adversaries like Russia and China, administration and congressional officials say.

stories.headline: Prince Harry visits Kyiv to back Ukraine and spotlight mine clearance
stories.summary: Ukraine welcomed Prince Harry to Kyiv on Thursday 23 April 2026 for a two day visit that remained unannounced until his arrival.

stories.headline: PM Sanchez rebuffs US idea of suspending Spain from NATO
stories.summary: PM Sanchez responded to reports that the US may suspend Madrid from NATO over its opposition to the war on Iran.

stories.headline: Why is this game only legal across Australia one day a year?
stories.summary: Two-up is a coin toss betting game that was played by Australian soldiers during World War One - it’s now only legal across Australia on 25 April.

stories.headline: Brazil Setting Higher Ethanol Blend to Stem Fuel Impact From War

stories.summary:
Todd Harrison, Founding Partner & Chief Investment Officer at CB1 Capital said the reclassification of marijuana as less dangerous was the biggest news for the cannabis industry since Nixon. 

The US Justice Department reclassified state-regulated marijuana as a less dangerous drug, a major shift that could increase legal cannabis sales and help a struggling industry.
The directive signed by acting Attorney General Todd Blanche moved licensed mar

stories.headline: Brazil Blocks Polymarket in Crackdown on Prediction Sites
stories.summary: Brazil’s government blocked access to Polymarket and two dozen other prediction market platforms that it said were out of compliance with federal gambling laws.

stories.headline: Nasdaq 100 Heads for Record After Intel’s Blockbuster Guidance
stories.summary: The Nasdaq 100 Index was on track for a fresh all-time closing high on Friday as a blockbuster sales forecast from Intel Corp. fueled a rally in technology stocks. Meanwhile, traders optimism about the Middle East increased as Iran’s foreign minister said he was headed to Pakistan.

stories.headline: Sonrava Health Hires Bank To Explore Asset Sales Following Lender Talks
stories.summary: Sonrava Health, an operator of dental clinics across the country, has hired investment bank Lincoln International LLC to explore options including asset sales, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Baker Hughes | Outlook Despite Middle East Disruption
stories.summary: Baker Hughes beats on earnings, sees outlook hold despite Mideast disruption. Lorenzo Simonelli, president and CEO of Baker Hughes, says the “outlook for the business fundamentals remains unchanged,” excluding the ongoing impacts in the Middle East. He spoke with Dani Burger and Matt Miller on Open Interest. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Tariffs Raised Consumers’ Prices, but the Refunds Go Only to Businesses

stories.summary: In 2024, the president made significant inroads with Hispanics. But in a competitive Arizona congressional district, voters now express dismay at his feud with the pope.

stories.summary: Mr. Blanche’s actions are meant to demonstrate progress on the president’s priorities, chief among them payback.

stories.headline: Woman killed by bear in Polish forest, son and local government say

stories.headline: Gold Gains on Iran Talks Optimism With Fed Outlook in Focus

stories.summary: Hopes that the US and Iran will be back to the negotiating table drove stocks toward all-time highs, with the market also rising after a blowout forecast from Intel Corp. and the closing of a probe into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

stories.headline: Trump intends to invite Putin to G-20 summit in Miami, officials say
